title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The drain pump is not operating correctly or water is not draining from the tub.
fullDescription: Error C1 indicates the dishwasher's drain pump cannot remove water from the tub. The control board detects standing water remains after the drain cycle. This can be caused by a blocked drain path, failed pump motor, or drain hose issues.

causes.cause: Drain pump is clogged with food debris or broken glass
Drain hose is kinked or clogged
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed
Drain pump motor has failed

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the tub

fixSteps.title: Check and clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack. Remove the fine filter and coarse filter from the bottom of the tub. Clean all debris, food particles, and grease under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks. If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal to clear it. Check that the knockout plug was removed from new disposals.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump from underneath. Check the impeller for blockages. Ensure it spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump does not run, test motor resistance (typical 5-20 ohms). Replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve C1 and the pump motor is failed, a professional can replace it efficiently.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C1 drain error fix

faq.q: What causes C1 most often?
faq.a: The most common causes are clogged filters and a garbage disposal knockout plug that was not removed during installation.

faq.q: How do I remove the disposal knockout plug?
faq.a: Insert a screwdriver into the disposal's dishwasher inlet and tap with a hammer to knock out the plastic plug. Remove the plug from inside the disposal.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes. Bail out water with a cup and soak up the remainder with towels or a wet-dry vacuum.

title: Drain Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not drain the water within the maximum allowed time.
fullDescription: Error C2 is related to C1 but specifically indicates a drain timeout. The drain pump may be running but water is not being removed quickly enough, suggesting a partial blockage in the drain path rather than a complete failure.

causes.cause: Partial blockage in the drain hose
Drain air gap or high loop is blocked
Drain pump impeller is partially blocked
Check valve in drain line is partially stuck

fixSteps.title: Clean filters thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filters under running water. Use a brush to remove grease buildup.

fixSteps.title: Flush the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose at the disposal/drain connection. Run water through it to check flow. Clear any partial blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check the air gap or high loop
fixSteps.instruction: If you have a countertop air gap, remove the cap and clean inside. If using a high loop, ensure the hose reaches at least 20 inches from the floor.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump from underneath. Remove and clean the impeller housing of any grease or small debris that may be restricting flow.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the entire drain path does not resolve C2, a professional can check for deeper blockages or pump issues.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C2 drain timeout error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between C1 and C2?
faq.a: C1 means the pump is not draining at all, while C2 means draining is too slow. C2 often indicates a partial blockage.

faq.q: What is a dishwasher air gap?
faq.a: An air gap is a small device mounted on the sink or countertop that prevents dirty sink water from flowing back into the dishwasher.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain path?
faq.a: Clean filters after every cycle. Flush the drain hose and check the air gap every 6 months.

title: Wash Pump Error
shortDescription: The main wash (circulation) pump is not operating properly.
fullDescription: Error C3 indicates the wash pump (circulation pump) that sprays water through the spray arms is not functioning correctly. The control board detects the pump is not starting, running at the wrong speed, or drawing abnormal current.

causes.cause: Wash pump motor has failed
Pump impeller is jammed by debris
Pump motor capacitor has failed
Wiring to the pump is damaged

fixSteps.title: Clean filters and check for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all filters and check the sump area for debris that could be blocking the wash pump impeller.

fixSteps.title: Verify spray arm rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the spray arms by hand. They should move freely. If blocked, clean the spray arm nozzles and check for food buildup at the mounting points.

fixSteps.title: Test the wash pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump from underneath. Disconnect and test resistance (typical 5-15 ohms). Check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is faulty, disconnect hoses, wiring, and mounting hardware. Install the new pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: Wash pump replacement is a significant repair. Professional installation is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C3 wash pump error fix

faq.q: Can a broken glass cause C3?
faq.a: Yes, broken glass can jam the wash pump impeller. Always check for broken items after running a load.

faq.q: How much does a GE dishwasher wash pump cost?
faq.a: The pump assembly typically costs $70-$160 for the part, plus $100-$240 for professional installation.

faq.q: Is C3 worth repairing?
faq.a: If the dishwasher is less than 8 years old, yes. The pump replacement cost ($180-$400) is significantly less than a new dishwasher.

title: Fill Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher did not fill with enough water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error C4 indicates the dishwasher could not fill with adequate water within the timeout period. The water level sensor does not detect sufficient water despite the fill valve being activated.

causes.cause: Water supply valve is not fully open
Inlet valve filter screen is clogged
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Float switch is stuck in the up position

symptoms: No water flow sound during fill

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply valve under the sink is fully open. Run the kitchen faucet to confirm water flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve screen
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water. Disconnect the supply line from the inlet valve. Remove and clean the small mesh filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Check float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door. Locate the float assembly on the tub floor. Lift and lower it. It should move freely. Clean around its base if stuck.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the valve solenoid (500-1500 ohms normal). Replace if open or shorted.

whenToCallPro: If supply and valve are fine, the control board or pressure sensor may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C4 not filling water error fix

faq.q: Why does my GE dishwasher not fill with water?
faq.a: The most common cause is a closed supply valve or clogged inlet screen. Start by checking these simple items.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause C4?
faq.a: Yes, if household water pressure is below 20 PSI, the dishwasher may not fill quickly enough.

faq.q: What is the float switch?
faq.a: A float switch is a simple device on the tub floor that rises with water level. If it gets stuck up, the dishwasher thinks it is full and will not fill.

title: Water Temperature Error
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor is not reading correctly or water is not reaching the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error C5 indicates a problem with the water temperature sensing or heating system. The dishwasher may not be able to heat water to the required temperature for proper cleaning and sanitizing.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed
Heating element has burned out
Incoming water temperature is too cold
Control board heating circuit has failed

fixSteps.title: Run hot water first
fixSteps.instruction: Run the kitchen faucet until the water is hot before starting the dishwasher. This ensures hot water reaches the dishwasher quickly.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thermistor in the sump area. Disconnect and measure resistance at room temperature. Compare to the service manual specification.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater and measure resistance (10-30 ohms typical). Check for ground faults between element and chassis.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the thermistor or heating element as needed.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and heater test fine, the control board heating relay needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C5 temperature heating error fix

faq.q: What temperature should dishwasher water be?
faq.a: For optimal cleaning, incoming water should be at least 120F (49C). The dishwasher's heater boosts it further for sanitizing.

faq.q: Can cold incoming water cause C5?
faq.a: Yes, if the hot water heater is set too low or the dishwasher is far from the heater, incoming water may be too cold for the dishwasher to heat in time.

faq.q: How do I test my water temperature?
faq.a: Run hot water at the kitchen faucet into a glass. Place a kitchen thermometer in the water. It should read at least 120F.

title: Diverter Motor Error
shortDescription: The water diverter motor that switches flow between spray zones is not operating.
fullDescription: Error C6 indicates the diverter motor (which directs water between the upper spray arm, lower spray arm, and other spray zones) is not responding to commands. Without proper water distribution, cleaning will be uneven.

causes.cause: Diverter motor has failed
Diverter valve is stuck due to debris
Wiring to the diverter motor is damaged
Control board output for diverter has failed

symptoms: Only top or bottom rack gets clean
Clicking sound from under the tub

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes. The diverter may need to recalibrate its position.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty cycle with dishwasher cleaner to dissolve any debris that may be binding the diverter valve.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: Access the diverter valve in the sump area. Check that it moves freely and is not jammed with food or scale.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor has failed, disconnect wiring and mounting hardware. Install the new motor.

whenToCallPro: If the motor tests fine, the control board may not be sending the command, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C6 diverter motor error fix

faq.q: Why does only the top rack get clean?
faq.a: The diverter valve may be stuck sending water only to the upper spray arm. C6 confirms the diverter is malfunctioning.

faq.q: Does every GE dishwasher have a diverter valve?
faq.a: Most modern GE dishwashers with multiple spray zones have a diverter valve. Older or basic models may not.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with C6?
faq.a: The dishwasher may still run but cleaning will be uneven since water is not being properly distributed to all spray zones.

title: Turbidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The soil/turbidity sensor that measures water clarity is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error C7 indicates the turbidity sensor (which measures how clean or dirty the wash water is) has failed or is reading incorrectly. This sensor is used to optimize wash cycle duration and water usage.

causes.cause: Turbidity sensor is coated with grease or residue
Turbidity sensor has failed
Sensor wiring is damaged
Control board sensor input has failed

symptoms: Water and energy use seems high

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the turbidity sensor in the sump area. Clean the lens with a soft cloth and mild detergent.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run a hot empty cycle with dishwasher cleaner to remove grease from the entire system.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve C7, disconnect the sensor and test its electrical output per the service manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor and install the new one. Ensure the lens is clean before installation.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and replacing the sensor does not clear C7, the control board input may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C7 turbidity sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: It measures water clarity by shining light through the water. Clearer water means cleaner dishes. The dishwasher uses this to optimize cycle time.

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without the turbidity sensor?
faq.a: You can use non-auto cycles that run for fixed times. However, the error should still be resolved for optimal performance.

faq.q: How does grease affect the sensor?
faq.a: Grease on the sensor lens blocks light, making the sensor think the water is always dirty, causing excessively long cycles.

title: Detergent Dispenser Error
shortDescription: The detergent dispenser mechanism has failed to operate properly.
fullDescription: Error C8 indicates the detergent dispenser motor or mechanism is not functioning. The dispenser should open at a specific point in the cycle to release detergent. If it cannot open, detergent is not dispensed into the wash water.

causes.cause: Dispenser latch or motor has failed
Detergent residue is jamming the dispenser
Dispenser wiring is damaged
Wax motor for dispenser has failed

symptoms: Detergent cup remains closed after cycle
Undissolved detergent pod found in dispenser
Dishes not clean despite using detergent
Dispenser door does not snap open during cycle

fixSteps.title: Clean the dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any dried detergent residue from the dispenser cup and its hinge/latch area. Use warm water and a brush to dissolve buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test the dispenser mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Close the dispenser and run a cycle. Listen for the click of the dispenser opening. If no click, the motor or wax actuator may have failed.

fixSteps.title: Check the actuator
fixSteps.instruction: Access the dispenser from the door interior. Test the wax motor or solenoid for continuity. Also check the bi-metal release if applicable.

fixSteps.title: Replace the dispenser assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the mechanism has failed, remove the inner door panel, disconnect the old dispenser, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the dispenser mechanism tests fine, the control board may not be sending the open signal.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher C8 detergent dispenser error fix

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without the dispenser?
faq.a: As a temporary workaround, you can place a detergent pod in the bottom of the tub. However, timing of detergent release will not be optimal.

faq.q: Why does detergent build up in the dispenser?
faq.a: Using too much detergent, running low-temperature cycles, or not rinsing dishes can cause residue buildup that eventually jams the dispenser.

faq.q: Should I use pods or liquid detergent?
faq.a: Both work well. Pods go in the dispenser cup (or the tub floor if dispenser is broken). Liquid or powder can also be used in the dispenser cup.

title: Power Failure Detected
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected a power interruption during a cycle.
fullDescription: Error PF indicates the dishwasher lost power during an active cycle and has now been restored. This is an informational code that tells you the cycle was interrupted by a power outage or the dishwasher was accidentally unplugged.

causes.cause: Power outage occurred during a cycle
Circuit breaker tripped
Loose electrical connection at the junction box
Shared circuit with high-draw appliance causing voltage drop

symptoms: Water may remain in the tub

fixSteps.title: Press Start to resume or cancel
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start button to resume the interrupted cycle, or press Cancel to drain the water and reset. The dishwasher will pick up where it left off or start fresh.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: If no area-wide outage occurred, check the circuit breaker panel. Reset any tripped breakers. Investigate why it tripped.

fixSteps.title: Check electrical connections
fixSteps.instruction: If PF happens frequently without power outages, check the junction box connection under the dishwasher for loose wire nuts or corroded wires.

fixSteps.title: Verify dedicated circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dishwasher is on its own dedicated circuit. Sharing with a garbage disposal or other appliance can cause voltage issues.

whenToCallPro: If PF occurs repeatedly without actual power outages, an electrician should check the circuit and junction box connections.
videoSearchQuery: GE dishwasher PF power failure error reset

faq.q: Do I need to restart the whole cycle after PF?
faq.a: No. Press Start to resume where the cycle left off, or Cancel to drain and start over.

faq.q: Will PF damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: No. PF is an informational code. The dishwasher is designed to handle power interruptions safely.

faq.q: Why does PF keep appearing?
faq.a: If it happens frequently without actual outages, you may have a loose electrical connection, a tripping breaker, or a shared circuit problem.
